items.title: F1 — Collapse the deprecated derived TOOL_EMOJI / PRIMARY_ARG_KEY maps and getToolEmoji
items.reason: Removing the @deprecated TOOL_EMOJI/PRIMARY_ARG_KEY/getToolEmoji/DEFAULT_TOOL_EMOJI exports is a breaking change to a published package surface; a human must confirm no out-of-tree/Discord-gateway consumer before removal (M02 expand/contract).
items.technique: T16 collapse premature/duplicate abstraction (DE-abstraction) via M02 expand/contract

items.title: F4 — Redirect leaky getToolEmoji internal call site to resolveToolPresenter(name,{}).emoji
items.reason: Not provably behavior-preserving: empty-{} resolution changes results for function-matched tools (e.g. command_execution would shift from ⚙️ to 💻); requires characterization tests pinning getHrcEventIcon across presenter families BEFORE the swap.
items.technique: T07 align interface to usage (redirect internal call to canonical resolver) + char-test rung

items.title: T07 — Narrow the over-broad public re-export surface of index.ts
items.reason: index.ts re-exports ~25 symbols but only 4 are consumed off-package (formatToolLine, formatNoticeLine, renderMarkdownBlock, admissionLabel); contracting the surface is a public-surface change needing human confirmation of no external consumers.
items.technique: T07 narrow leaky interface via M02 expand/contract

items.title: F5 — admissionLabelFromResponse if-chain → table-driven dispatch
items.reason: Report explicitly declines it: lateral change, not a win — the explicit if-ordering IS the precedence spec (ambiguous before reason before queued) and is easy to read; a rule table can obscure precedence. Left for completeness only.
items.technique: T19 conditional → dispatch (ordered rule list)

items.title: F2 — Remove `attachOpenAiRuntime` pure pass-through middle-man
items.reason: It lives on the documented `cli.ts` re-export surface and is referenced only by `cli.test.ts`; collapsing it requires editing the test and the public re-export block, which the safe contract excludes.
items.technique: [T23] remove middle man / collapse pass-through; delete the function + its `cli.ts` re-export and repoint `cli.test.ts` onto `attachWithRetry`

items.title: F3 — Hand-rolled `parseArgv` flag loop duplicates intent across two monitor commands
items.reason: The two parsers throw byte-exact error strings asserted by monitor-wait.acceptance.test.ts and use two divergent error models (CliUsageError vs argv.ts fatal); converging them is easy to get subtly wrong and unverifiable without running the monitor suites.
items.technique: [T15] extract the shared `--name value`/`--name=value` matcher (matchStringFlag + the two integer parsers) into a private `monitor-args.ts` consumed by `parseWaitArgs`; keep error bytes identical; do NOT fold in `cli/argv.ts`'s `parseFlag`

items.title: F4 — Event-filter predicate is encoded three times
items.reason: `buildEventFilter` (in-memory), `deriveStoreFilters` (SQL), and `isFilterActive` must stay in lockstep; the SQL-vs-in-memory parity is a stated invariant (T-04232) and a refactor that drifts them is a regression I cannot validate without running that suite.
items.technique: [T15]/[T21] introduce `normalizeEventFilterSpec(args) → FilterSpec | undefined` once and derive the active-flag, the in-memory predicate, and the store-filter from that single object (preserve both consumers' existence — the in-memory predicate is a deliberate redundant guard)

items.title: F4 — Parallel switch(condition) dispatch tables (evaluateStartSnapshot / evaluateEvent) must be kept congruent
items.reason: Report recommends leave-as-is absent a third parallel switch; TS already enforces per-function exhaustiveness via the union return, so the only gain is co-locating start/event halves — a judgment-call restructure a human should weigh.
items.technique: [T19] conditional -> dispatch: per-condition strategy record { start, event } keyed by HrcMonitorCondition with `satisfies Record<HrcMonitorCondition, ...>`

items.title: F5 — protectStreamCursor Proxy: 25 lines of get/set/defineProperty traps to make one numeric field read-only
items.reason: Requires a characterization test pinning spread/JSON/enumerable + assignment-rejection behavior BEFORE simplifying (the Proxy may guard a real past cursor-mutation bug per the :165 monotonic-cursor comment); whole-object Object.freeze is explicitly rejected. Human must author the char-test and only swap mechanism (Proxy -> defineProperty/frozen single field), not remove protection.
items.technique: [T16] collapse premature abstraction: replace Proxy with Object.defineProperty({writable:false}) on streamCursorSeq, gated by a new char-test

items.title: Public type surface (index.ts) — ~150 exported wire/RPC contract types are deliberately wide; any [T07] narrowing or [M02] shape change is a breaking change for 180+ downstream consumers across 5 packages (several consume built dist/*.d.ts)
items.file: packages/hrc-core/src/index.ts (re-exports of contracts.ts, http-contracts.ts, hrcchat-contracts.ts, monitor/*, errors.ts, selectors.ts)
items.reason: Boundary is healthy and load-bearing; restructuring exported types is a redesign requiring an expand/contract migration across hrc-server (119), hrc-cli (22), hrcchat-cli (17), hrc-store-sqlite (16), hrc-sdk (6) — not a refactor-pass edit. A human must own any such migration.
items.technique: [M02] expand/contract migration (additive-optional only), NOT in-place narrowing

items.title: F1 [T10] Reify the implicit assistant-segment state machine
items.reason: Extracting an AssistantSegmentBuffer touches the central 460-line reducer with 4 call-sites that have subtly different targetRef ?? currentAssistantMessageRef fallbacks (lines 338 vs 378); needs char-tests added first to prove no behavior drift before churning, which is outside a no-build safe pass.
items.technique: extract missing abstraction (AssistantSegmentBuffer with startMessage/appendDelta/replaceBody/closeActive owning assistantSegments + activeAssistantSegmentId + currentAssistantMessageRef)

items.title: F3 [T22] Guard-clause / hoist the repeated `if (!runId) break` per-case prologue
items.reason: Hoisting the 7 run-context guards requires keeping getAffectedRunId (940-956) classification in perfect sync with the dispatcher; best done together with F1's per-event handlers, and the central reducer needs char-tests first — too risky to do blind under the no-build constraint.
items.technique: collapse/flatten nesting — resolve effective runId once before the switch (or pass resolved run into per-event handlers)

items.title: F5 [T16] `hrcLifecycleEventToSessionEnvelope` alias is a pure pass-through of `adaptHrcLifecycleEvent`
items.reason: Removing the alias is a published-export contract break (M02); doc comments name gateway-discord as an out-of-repo consumer, so a human must confirm no external import before dropping it.
items.technique: remove middle man / collapse duplicate public name

items.title: F6 [T15] Hoist the `isRecord(payload) ? payload : {}` record-guard across adapt* functions
items.reason: A single-call-site recordOrEmpty helper adds indirection with no dedup payoff; the real value needs it applied across adapt* (lines 93,113,143,199,264) where guards return DIFFERENT sentinels (undefined vs {}), and a shared helper must not flatten that distinction — judged unsafe to do in isolation.
items.technique: extract small private helper recordOrEmpty(payload) applied uniformly across adapters (preserving per-function sentinel semantics)

items.title: F7 [T01] `createLogger` re-reads `process.env['LOG_LEVEL']` on every write
items.reason: Caching the threshold at logger/module creation is a behavior change for LOG_LEVEL mutated mid-process (no longer takes effect); violates the strict behavior-preserving contract even though no current test mutates env, so left for a human to decide the seam (cache vs optional level arg).
items.technique: seam — compute threshold once at createLogger time, or accept an optional level field in the arg object

items.title: T07 narrow leaky boundary — onRender 5th arg `run: RunState`
items.reason: OnRenderCallback is an exported type; dropping the 5th run arg changes the published signature (the sole in-repo consumer ignores it) but may strand an out-of-repo sink — M02 expand/contract requiring human consumer knowledge.
items.technique: narrow exported callback signature (drop run: RunState param) after confirming no external consumer

items.title: T16 collapse speculative generality — sink metadata + onRunQueued callback + unused helper/type exports
items.file: packages/hrc-frame-render/src/session-events-manager.ts:799-957 (setSinkMetadata 888-896, getRunState 880-882, onRunQueued 799/804/923-925, sinkMetadata field types.ts/index.ts) + hrc-event-adapter.ts:51 canonicalSessionRefFromEvent + Gateway*Event/PermissionAction/RunId/ProjectId exports
items.reason: setSinkMetadata, getRunState, sinkMetadata field, onRunQueued ctor param+callback, canonicalSessionRefFromEvent, hrcLifecycleEventToSessionEnvelope and all Gateway*Event type exports have zero in-repo callers but the doc comments name gateway-discord; removing any published export is an M02 contract break needing a human to confirm no external/future sink.
items.technique: surface narrowing — remove unconsumed exports/ctor params/fields after confirming zero external consumers (expand/contract migration)

items.title: sendInFlightInput manual spread duplicates deprecated input/prompt plumbing
items.reason: Dropping the `@deprecated input` field is a wire-contract contraction; the server's `prompt ?? input` fallback exists for older/out-of-repo consumers, so it requires an expand/contract deprecation cycle not a unilateral edit.
items.technique: B/M02 contract contraction (collapse dual input/prompt to canonical prompt across releases)

items.title: getStatus/listTargets/watch repeat the `? 'true' : undefined` boolean-flag idiom, inconsistent with buildPath's String(boolean)
items.reason: Two flag conventions coexist (`'true'`/absent vs `'true'`/`'false'`); unifying mechanically would silently change request query strings, so a human must confirm each endpoint's server-side parser before picking one convention.
items.technique: C/T15 extract a `boolField` helper after confirming server parsing for status/targets/runtimes flags

items.title: F1 — Contract narrowing: drop unused repository-class value exports from index.ts
items.reason: These are published symbols across 121 consumers; removal requires surface-owner (hrc-server/hrc-cli) sign-off and a full monorepo tsc, plus confirmation no external migration/repair tool constructs a bare repository.
items.technique: DE-abstraction / M02 expand-contract on the published interface
items.effort: S (deletion + monorepo typecheck), but gated on owner approval

items.title: F2 — Remove middle man: collapse the repositories.ts re-export barrel
items.file: packages/hrc-store-sqlite/src/repositories.ts:1-70 (consumed by database.ts:28, index.ts:16/25, and two in-package tests)
items.reason: Behavior-preserving but a deliberate maintainer-preference contraindication — the single barrel is load-bearing as documentation/one-import-source; the report says collapse only IF the team agrees it isn't wanted, so it needs a human decision.
items.technique: DE-abstraction (remove pass-through barrel; rewire imports to ./repositories/*.js)

items.title: F4 — Two-tier nullable-transform inconsistency in patch specs
items.reason: Stripping a redundant `nullableTransform` is unsafe without auditing each column's patch-field type — for any field typed `T | null | undefined` the transform is load-bearing (explicit null must coerce to SQL NULL); a wrong removal silently turns an intended null-write into a no-op.
items.technique: simplify (drop redundant transform) — per-column patch-type audit + char-test asserting null still writes NULL

items.title: F5 — Merge markAccepted/markRejected thin wrappers
items.reason: Both bodies are identical delegations to markResponse, but collapsing them requires renaming the methods and updating external call sites in hrc-server (sdk-turn-handlers.ts:449,451) — outside this package's edit scope; and the two names are intent-revealing at the call site.
items.technique: collapse pass-through methods + update hrc-server call sites

items.title: F6 — findByHostSessionId is a pure alias of getByHostSessionId
items.reason: Removing the alias requires updating an external call site in hrc-cli tests (cli.test.ts:2024) — outside this package's edit scope; cannot be completed cleanly here without touching another dir.
items.technique: dedup synonym method + update hrc-cli call site to getByHostSessionId

items.title: Divergent duplicate isTurnEnd (two definitions, different semantics)
items.reason: The two predicates disagree (turn.ts: turn_end||turn.completed; aggregator: turn.completed only); unifying is a behavior decision that must be confirmed against the live broker event stream before collapsing.
items.technique: T15 extract missing abstraction (single terminal-event predicate into stacked-shared.ts/event-kinds.ts)

items.title: Repeated aggregator.finish(...) + throw TurnExitError terminal blocks
items.apiImpact: internal-only (exit codes 1/4/5/etc are the user-facing CLI contract)
items.reason: A TERMINALS dispatch table must preserve every (phase,exitCode,result,message) triple exactly; arms are not byte-identical and per-arm exit-code test coverage must be confirmed first (e.g. TURN_EXIT_RUNTIME_DEAD reused with differing Results).
items.technique: T19 conditional → dispatch table + single finalizeTurn(kind) helper

items.title: extractTaskId / task-id parsing duplicated across modules
items.reason: Relocation-only (two genuinely distinct grammars: regex-on-scope vs colon-split-on-sessionRef); marginal value and merging the grammars would be wrong, so a human should decide whether the shared taskId.ts module is worth the indirection.
items.technique: T03 relocate by affinity (shared taskId.ts exposing taskIdFromScope + taskIdFromSessionRef)

items.title: F3 — checkPreStartDriverCapabilities ~= checkInvocationCapabilities near-duplication
items.reason: The two divergent arms (input.queue requiredOnly-wrap vs passthrough; turns.concurrency === 'multiple' vs !== 'any') are behaviorally load-bearing pre-start-vs-post-start policy and the caps shapes differ (DriverSummary['capabilities'] vs InvocationCapabilities); needs a characterization test pinning the exact missing[] for one admitting + one rejecting profile through BOTH functions before unifying the 11 identical checkNeed arms.
items.technique: extract shared checkCommonCapabilities(missing, requirements, caps) for the 11 identical checkNeed calls; keep the 2 divergent arms in each caller

items.title: F6 — markStartedInvocationFailed/failReplayStale/markBrokerCrashTerminal repeat the runtimeStateJson spread-and-stamp
items.reason: The terminal runtimeStateJson is observed by hrc run shutdown-report + inspect surface and the field set differs per terminal kind (terminalReason+terminalInvocation vs lastAttachError vs brokerCrash/admissionFailure); a spread-merge helper risks normalizing key order or dropping a conditional key, so it requires a char-test pinning the exact before/after JSON per call site first.
items.technique: extract applyTerminalRuntimeState(db, runtime, {status, now, diagnostic}) preserving the exact per-call field set

items.title: F5 — isRecord type-guard triplicated across the module
items.reason: Report recommends skip/defer: these are intentionally local leaf utilities and a shared import adds a cross-file dependency edge with low standalone value; only worth doing bundled with F6's endpoint consolidation, which is itself deferred.
items.technique: export one isRecord from a single leaf (event-mapper/helpers.ts or a new broker/json.ts) and import it

items.title: F7 — connectDurableBrokerWithRetry 'unreachable' final throw (partial->total)
items.reason: Report recommends leave alone: the current post-loop throw documents the retry budget and is correct defensive code; re-expressing the loop to delete the dead tail is churn with negative clarity value.
items.technique: re-express as for(attempt of 1..max-1){retry}; return await lastAttempt() to make totality compiler-evident

items.title: DF-1 — notActive check widening: awaited RPC can resolve against a since-deleted active record (concurrency)
items.reason: The not-active check is read at call entry but the awaited broker RPC can resolve after the active record is deleted; closing this gap is a concurrency/ownership semantics change (not behavior-preserving) and must be designed + tested by a human, not folded into a mechanical refactor.
items.technique: re-validate active ownership after the await, or hold a per-runtime ownership token across the RPC

items.title: DF-2 — duplicated durable broker endpoint type across the module
items.reason: The duplicated endpoint type is observable on the public/inspect surface (pinned by broker-substrate-presentation-characterization.test.ts); consolidating it touches an externally-pinned type and needs a human to confirm no contract drift across runtime-hosting predicates.
items.technique: consolidate the endpoint type into one source and re-point runtime-hosting/runtime-state imports

items.title: Finding 1 [T16]: collapse premature abstraction — un-export the two single-caller broker-profile predicates
items.reason: isHeadlessCodexBrokerProfile / isInteractiveTmuxBrokerProfile are imported directly by tests (compile-profile-selector.test.ts, headless-v02-admission.red.test.ts), so dropping export is a test-visible change requiring those tests to be updated and a human call on whether the granular per-rule exports are intended.
items.technique: DE-abstraction: drop export to make module-private (or inline into isBrokerControllerProfile)

items.title: Finding 2 [T15]: extract the `...(x !== undefined ? { x } : {})` optional-spread idiom into an optional() helper
items.reason: The spread feeds hashed/projected material (jsonEqual/recomputeStartRequestHash); a subtle {k: undefined} regression is invisible until a hash mismatch, and the report requires gating on byte-identical JSON.stringify which cannot be verified here without running builds/tests.
items.technique: extract a tiny optional<K,V>(k,v) helper preserving the exact-field-set (key absent when undefined) invariant

items.title: Finding 3 [T17]: toFrontend silently coerces unknown frontends to 'agent-sdk'
items.reason: Replacing the silent default with a typed HrcUnprocessableEntityError(PROVIDER_MISMATCH) or an exhaustive type changes observable behavior on the error branch; the 'anthropic is the safe default' policy may be deliberate and needs a human decision.
items.technique: partial→total: emit/throw on unexpected frontend, or make the union exhaustive so the fallback is provably dead

items.title: Finding 4 (full form) [T18]: classify the missing-active-run retry condition on a typed signal instead of a substring
items.reason: Switching from message.includes() to a structured error code/subclass requires confirming agent-spaces actually exposes such a code and adding a characterization test pinning the real upstream error (server-inflight.test.ts already constructs the literal message) — the reduced constant-extraction was applied, but the typed-classification + pinning test is the remaining human work.
items.technique: reify implicit contract: switch to a typed error code/subclass from agent-spaces, add a characterization test against the real upstream error

items.title: Finding 5 [T15]: dedupe the aspHome/spec/cwd placement-placeholder triad into a shared placementPlaceholders() helper
items.reason: The two sites live in different files and feed different agent-spaces request types (RunTurnNonInteractiveRequest vs BuildProcessInvocationSpecRequest); a shared helper introduces new cross-file adapter coupling for three trivial fields that a reviewer should bless, and the report itself rates it only 'borderline auto-applicable'.
items.technique: extract a shared placementPlaceholders() returning { aspHome: getAspHome(), spec: { spaces: [] }, cwd: '/' } spread into both request builders along with the shared explanatory comment

items.title: F2 — Collapse parseTerminateRuntimeRequest stringField closure into readOptionalNonEmptyStringField
items.reason: The local closure allows empty-string and does NOT trim reason/source/actor; readOptionalNonEmptyStringField rejects empty and trims, so swapping is a semantic/behavior change that needs characterization tests pinning whitespace/empty handling first.
items.technique: DE-duplicate -> reuse existing abstraction (collapse one-off closure)

items.title: F2 (related) — Replace inline optional-non-empty-string projections with readOptionalNonEmptyStringField
items.reason: Same trim/empty semantic drift risk as the terminate closure for the reason projection (and similar inline string projections); must verify no consumer distinguishes '' from absent before collapsing.
items.technique: DE-duplicate -> reuse existing abstraction

items.title: F5 — Extract requireOneOf / parseEnumField for repeated enum-membership chains
items.reason: Behavior-preserving in principle, but the report's own contraindication warns TS will not narrow the value to the union literal after extraction; needs a generic + as-const tuple and a biome useLiteralKeys/narrowing check that downstream assignments (e.g. transport: transport) type-check without casts — not safe to apply blind without a build/lint gate (which this pass is forbidden from running). Touches duplicated transport check in runtime.ts:62 and sweeps.ts:17, restartStyle in runtime.ts:257 and app-sessions.ts:273, provider in runtime.ts:129, launchMode in app-sessions.ts:98, spec.kind in app-sessions.ts:194.
items.technique: DE-duplicate -> extract + conditional->dispatch (table)

items.title: F8 — Extract requireSelector preamble + unify divergent missing-selector error
items.reason: Six functions throw 'selector is required' while parseInterruptAppSessionRequest (:455) and parseTerminateAppSessionRequest (:467) fall through to 'selector must be an object'; unifying changes an observable error string on those two endpoints — a behavior change that must be deliberately chosen and pinned by tests, not applied as a safe internal refactor.
items.technique: DE-duplicate -> extract; [T17] partial->total (unify missing-selector behavior)

items.title: F3 — Thin pass-through parser wrappers (parseStartRuntimeRequest, parseInspectRuntimeRequest, parseAttachRuntimeRequest)
items.reason: Report pressure-tested and explicitly rejected collapsing these: each returns a distinct DTO type and is a named route-table seam; collapsing would erase the type distinction and force call-site casts. Recorded for completeness — no action recommended.
items.technique: [T23] remove middle man (REJECTED — keep)

items.title: F1 [T15] Extract the shared runtime-terminal-mutation skeleton (finalizeActiveRun + settleInvocation)
items.reason: markRuntime{Dead,Stale,Terminated} are re-exported via the barrel startup-reconcile.ts and asserted by characterization tests; consolidating their triplicated bodies needs the test gate run (forbidden here) to prove the stale/terminated/run-fail outcomes stay identical, and must preserve the deliberate runtimeStateJson key divergence.
items.technique: extract private helpers (finalizeActiveRun, settleInvocation) and have the three public mutators compose them while keeping their explicit status/runtimeStateJson/event tails

items.title: F3 [T22] Flatten the nested sweep loop in sweepOrphanedBrokerTmuxLeases (extract classifyLeaseSocket)
items.reason: Report gates this behind a six-counter sweep fixture (scanned/skippedClaimed/skippedWithinGrace/killedLiveLeaseServers/removedDeadSocketFiles/errors) and a stat-race-must-NOT-increment-errors invariant; the body has side effects (rm/killServer/writeServerLog) and two distinct try/catch layers, so extraction is unsafe without running that test gate (builds/tests forbidden in this pass).
items.technique: extract classifyLeaseSocket(entry) -> 'claimed'|'within-grace'|'vanished'|'dead'|'live-orphan' and flatten the loop to a dispatch, keeping the inner stat catch (vanished, no error++) separate from the outer catch (real error, error++)

items.title: F3 — Duplicated transport derivation for headless/sdk
items.reason: Only 2 occurrences of the `runtime.transport === 'headless' ? 'headless' : 'sdk'` ternary; below rule-of-three, and the report itself flags it borderline (fold-in-with-F2-or-skip). I deferred to keep the applied set minimal and avoid an under-justified one-line helper; a human can decide whether the tiny dedup is worth a named const helper.
items.technique: [T15] extract missing abstraction (typed const helper headlessAuditTransport(runtime))

items.title: F5 — terminateRuntime / interruptRuntime transport dispatch is a conditional ladder
items.reason: The report explicitly marks this DE-prioritized / not-a-finding: a dispatch table would force a uniform method signature the three per-transport terminators do not share (each forwards different opts), obscuring the plumbing. Listed for transparency only — left intentionally as-is, no action recommended unless a human deliberately reshapes the per-transport opts contracts first.
items.technique: [T19] conditional → dispatch (observe-only; contraindicated)

items.title: F4 — handleBrokerLiteralInputBySelector implicit 3-state dispatch: consolidate the event-emit + response epilogue across the buffer / empty-enter / dispatch arms
items.apiImpact: internal-only (events are internal but observable on the event stream; consumers key off the `delivery` tag)
items.reason: The three near-identical appendHrcEvent('target.literal-input', ...) blocks differ only by delivery tag / payloadLength / enter / runId and a deliberate timestamp() recomputation in the dispatch arm; folding them risks observable event-data drift, so it needs characterization tests on all three broker delivery paths (delivery tag + payloadLength + ts semantics) before a human consolidates the tail.
items.technique: T19 conditional→dispatch / T10 reify state machine (consolidate the shared event-emit + response epilogue; keep the three decision arms explicit)

items.title: F2 — `as BrokerWindowIdentity` / `as BrokerTmuxLease` casts mask a total-vs-partial guarantee
items.reason: Removing the two casts requires reshaping the EXPORTED BrokerSubstrateAllocation into a discriminated union on presentation.kind, which ripples to test fixtures/consumers (e.g. broker-headless-durable.red.test.ts:316) — a public-type redesign needing human sign-off; report itself recommends leaving as-is.
items.technique: [T17] partial→total — split BrokerSubstrateAllocation into a discriminated union on presentation.kind so the 'tmux-tui' variant has non-optional tuiWindow/tuiLease, letting the tmux adapter narrow without the two `as` casts.

items.title: Stale/over-wide barrel export of unused replay surface (replaySpoolEntries/parseReplayPayload/SpoolPostCallback/SpoolReplayResult)
items.reason: Removing the replay surface or narrowing the barrel requires confirming with the spool-test author whether the test characterizes intended capability (a spared socket-replay fallback) vs dead code; production uses replay-spool.ts against the DB, but this is a deliberate-spare judgment call.
items.technique: DE-abstraction (remove) or narrow barrel; [T07] align interface to usage / [T16] collapse premature abstraction

items.title: Barrel omits env/otel helpers that siblings deep-import (sanitizeTmux*, listInheritedEnvKeysToScrub, injectCodexOtelConfig)
items.file: packages/hrc-server/src/launch/index.ts (missing exports); bypass sites tmux.ts:5-8, codex-otel.ts callers
items.reason: Choosing between widening the barrel (and migrating tmux.ts + otel callers) vs formalizing a documented file-deep-import split is a human direction decision; either way it alters the module's declared public contract.
items.technique: widen leaky interface or formalize split; [T07] align interface to usage

items.title: F2 — Collapse premature abstraction: dead _deps param + BrainRuntimeResolver export
items.reason: BrainRuntimeResolver is re-exported via the brain-enricher.ts shim, so removing it (and the ignored _deps param/BrainEnricherDeps type that only exists to carry it) is a contract narrowing requiring an expand/contract pass; the param-only removal is internal but worthless without the export trim.
items.technique: de-abstract / remove unused injection seam (T16)

items.title: F3 — Orphaned modules format.ts and cache.ts are fully unreferenced dead code
items.reason: Deleting these silently erases the only gbrain formatting/caching implementation; it is staged WIP (commit 7786c6a) and the delete-vs-restore choice is a product decision a human must make, not a mechanical refactor.
items.technique: dead-code removal OR re-integration (T16)
items.effort: S to delete / L to restore

items.title: F4 — Partial→total: unreachable arms in the BrainEnricherResult.reason union
items.reason: Members enabled/injection-disabled/resolution-error/query-timeout are never produced today; narrowing the union is a public-surface contract narrowing (M02) coupled to the same restore-vs-delete decision as F2/F3, so a human must confirm enrichment is abandoned before trimming.
items.technique: narrow union to reachable states OR restore producing paths (T17)

items.title: F1 — Characterization tests on the public surface (gates all structural change)
items.reason: Adding tests is not one of the permitted behavior-preserving refactor techniques for this apply pass, and the report itself sequences a human product decision (restore vs delete) immediately after; a human should land these char-tests (3 reachable reasons + formatBrainPrompt/cacheKey edges) before any union/dead-code trim.
items.technique: make-safe / add characterization tests (T40)

items.title: HrcServerInstanceForHandlers is an any-typed mirror of the entire class surface
items.reason: ~150 handler members are typed via HandlerMethod = (...args: any[]) => any, erasing type safety across the whole cross-handler call graph. Re-typing to real method signatures (or inverting this to HrcServerInstance) is the right direction but has broad blast radius across every *-handlers.ts module and a mistyped this param breaks compilation widely; large effort, easy to get subtly wrong.
items.technique: T07 align interface to usage

items.title: planActiveRunReconcile status->action ladder is a hand-rolled state machine in the safety-critical reaper
items.reason: ~10 sequential status/launch/substrate if-branches map to distinct reap actions+error codes. The branch ORDERING is the invariant (ask-bracket T-01946 and per-runtime broker-socket liveness probe T-01941 must precede the generic status ladder, or a live parked/durable broker is wrongly reaped). Branches are non-uniform incl. an async side-effecting killServer probe. A table rewrite would re-encode precedence+async for little clarity gain and real regression risk in the most safety-critical path; behavior is observable via reap reason codes/events.
items.technique: T10 reify state machine

items.title: option-resolvers flag resolution near-identical 4x — parameterized resolveBooleanFlag helper
items.reason: Report tags it AUTO-APPLICABLE-but-optional and contraindicates: the default-ON (broker cutover flags) vs default-OFF (durable-IPC dark) asymmetry is intentional/load-bearing and a single {defaultOn} helper obscures it at each call site; net value marginal, recommended only if touching the file for another reason.
items.technique: T15/T21 extract parameterized helper with explicit defaultOn at call sites

items.title: HrcServerInstanceForHandlers is an any-typed surface mirror (HandlerMethod = (...args: any[]) => any over ~150 members)
items.apiImpact: internal-only (but pervasive — every handler module receives this: HrcServerInstanceForHandlers)
items.reason: Replacing the any-typed mirror with real method signatures (or inverting to this: HrcServerInstance) is the right direction but broad blast radius across every handler module; a mistyped `this` param breaks compilation repo-wide. Needs human-driven incremental migration with full typecheck gating.
items.technique: T07 align interface to usage — derive concrete method signatures or invert this-type

items.title: planActiveRunReconcile status→action ladder (hand-rolled decision table, ~10 sequential if-branches)
items.apiImpact: internal-only (behavior observable via reap reason codes / events)
items.reason: Branch ORDERING is the invariant: ask-bracket check (T-01946) and per-runtime broker-substrate socket probe (T-01941) MUST precede the generic status ladder or a live parked/durable broker is wrongly reaped; branch bodies are non-uniform (distinct error codes, async side-effecting killServer probe). Table-driven rewrite yields little clarity for real regression risk in the most safety-critical reaper path. Human judgment required.
items.technique: T10 reify state machine / T19 decision table
